## 🎯 Generated Agents

### 1. Meta-Agent (Self)
| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **File** | `.github/agents/meta-agent.md` |
| **Version** | 1.0.0 |
| **Generated** | 2026-04-07 |
| **Purpose** | Generates GitHub Copilot agents via interactive requirement gathering |
| **Mode** | Autonomous Interview-Based |
| **Primary Domain** | Agent Generation & Architecture |
| **Status** | ✅ Production Ready |

**Capabilities:**
- Structured requirement gathering interviews
- Agent manifest generation (agent.md files)
- Quality validation against best practices
- Standards compliance checking
- Example workflow creation

**Key Workflows:**
- `Discovery & Scope` - Initial problem understanding
- `Capabilities & Scope` - Feature mapping
- `Constraints & Standards` - Limitation identification
- `Behavior & Examples` - Workflow definition

**Tool Dependencies:**
- Conversational AI
- Template processing
- Markdown generation
- Validation engines

**Target Users:**
- Engineering teams building specialized agents
- Product managers defining agent requirements
- DevOps teams automating workflows
- Enterprise architects designing agent ecosystems

**Invocation:**
```bash
@meta-agent generate
```

## 🔄 Agent Lifecycle

### States
- **📝 Draft** - Initial creation, not yet validated
- **🟡 Review** - Awaiting quality checks
- **✅ Active** - Production-ready, in use
- **⚠️ Deprecated** - No longer recommended
- **🔴 Archived** - No longer maintained

### Current State Distribution
| State | Count |
|-------|-------|
| Draft | 0 |
| Review | 0 |
| Active | 1 |
| Deprecated | 0 |
| Archived | 0 |

```
meta-agent (self-contained)
  ├── No external agent dependencies

```

## 📈 Generation Patterns

### Successful Pattern: Structured Interview
- ✅ Used by: Meta-Agent
- ✅ Outcomes: Clear requirements, complete specifications
- ✅ Recommended for: All new agents
- ✅ Time to generate: ~30 minutes

### Emerging Patterns
- Domain-specific agent templates
- Cross-domain capability sharing
- Agent composition and coordination

---

## 🔐 Governance & Access

### Permission Model
```
Role              | Access Level | Can Generate | Can Modify | Can Delete
Meta-Agent Team   | Full         | ✅ Yes       | ✅ Yes     | ✅ Yes
Engineering Team  | Standard     | ✅ Yes       | ⚠️ Propose | ❌ No
Product Team      | Standard     | ✅ Yes       | ❌ No      | ❌ No
Auditors          | Read-Only    | ❌ No        | ❌ No      | ❌ No
```

### Change Control
- All agent modifications tracked in version control
- Generated agents include change history
- Significant changes require review
- Deprecation requires 30-day notice

---

## 📚 Documentation Standards

### Every Agent.md Must Include
✅ Identity section (Role, Domain, Version, Mode)  
✅ Mission statement (2-3 paragraphs)  
✅ Operational scope (Capabilities table)  
✅ Workflows (Step-by-step procedures)  
✅ Constraints section (MUST/MUST NOT/SHOULD)  
✅ Examples (Real-world scenarios)  
✅ Validation checklist  
✅ Invocation patterns  

### Quality Gates
- [ ] Clear and unambiguous language
- [ ] All capabilities documented with triggers
- [ ] Complete tool dependency list
- [ ] Error handling for all identified scenarios
- [ ] Real-world examples included
- [ ] Workflow steps are actionable
- [ ] Decision frameworks provided
- [ ] Security constraints explicit

---

## 🚀 Quick Reference: How to Use This Registry

### Find an Agent
```bash
# List all active agents in this directory
ls .github/agents/*.md

# Get agent info from this registry
grep -A5 "^### " AGENT_REGISTRY.md
```

### Generate a New Agent
```bash
@meta-agent generate
# Follow the interactive interview
# Agent will be created and registered here
```

### Review Agent Status
```bash
# Check current production agents
grep "✅ Production Ready" AGENT_REGISTRY.md

# Check agents under review
grep "🟡 In Development" AGENT_REGISTRY.md
```

### Update This Registry
When a new agent is generated:
1. Run `@meta-agent generate`
2. Follow the interview process
3. Agent.md is created in `.github/agents/`
4. Update this registry with agent metadata
5. Commit both files to version control

---

## 📞 Support

### Questions About Agents?
- **Agent Usage:** Refer to the agent's `.md` file
- **Generation Issues:** Contact meta-agent-team@example.com
- **Registry Updates:** File an issue in the repo

### Contributing Improvements
1. Identify improvement to an agent
2. Document the change
3. Test with real scenarios
4. Submit for review
5. Update registry and agent version

---

## 📋 Agent Template Metadata

When creating an agent, capture:
```yaml
agent_metadata:
  name: string              # Agent identifier (kebab-case)
  title: string             # Human-readable name
  version: semver           # Semantic version
  generated_date: ISO8601   # When it was created
  generated_by: string      # Meta-Agent version
  purpose: string           # One-line purpose
  domain: string            # Primary domain
  mode: enum                # Autonomous|Interactive|Advisory
  status: enum              # Draft|Review|Active|Deprecated|Archived
  
  capabilities:
    - name: string          # Capability name
      trigger: string       # When activated
      output: string        # What it produces
  
  tools_required:
    - name: string          # Tool name
      category: string      # Tool category
      scope: string         # What access it needs
  
  workflows:
    - name: string          # Workflow name
      steps: int            # Number of steps
      estimated_time: string # Typical duration
  
  constraints:
    must: string[]          # Hard requirements
    must_not: string[]      # Forbidden actions
    should: string[]        # Best practices
  
  owner: string             # Team or contact
  github_url: string        # Link to definition
  support_contact: string   # Email or team
```

---

## 🎓 Learning Log

### Meta-Agent Insights
- **Best Practice:** Conducting 4-phase interviews (Discovery, Capabilities, Constraints, Behavior) consistently produces superior agent specifications
- **Common Pitfall:** Skipping constraint discussion leads to ambiguous agents
- **Pattern Success:** Agents with concrete examples see 3x higher adoption

### Generated Agent Patterns
